rationale_for_baseline_tier: Acute mechanical large bowel obstruction (colicky pain, bilious vomiting, absolute constipation, distension) on a colorectal-cancer red-flag background, with intractable vomiting, inability to tolerate fluids and signs of dehydration. This is a time-critical surgical emergency threatening bowel ischaemia, perforation, septic shock and hypovolaemic collapse. The patient cannot tolerate oral intake and needs IV resuscitation, imaging and surgical assessment — T5 (call 000 for emergency ambulance to ED) is the only appropriate tier, consistent with the source disposition.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Colicky abdominal pain with bilious vomiting and no flatus/stool — suspected acute bowel obstruction, unable to keep fluids down
escalation_edges.rationale: Complete mechanical obstruction with vomiting and dehydration is a surgical emergency; delay risks ischaemia, perforation and shock.
escalation_edges.communication_template: This looks like a possible blockage in the bowel, which is an emergency. I'm going to ask you to call 000 for an ambulance now. Please don't give her anything to eat or drink.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Change in bowel habit + rectal bleeding + weight loss + fatigue in an older adult (colorectal-cancer red-flag pattern) presenting acutely
escalation_edges.rationale: The red-flag pattern makes the obstruction almost certainly malignant and reinforces the need for immediate hospital assessment rather than routine cancer work-up.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Because of the changes in her bowels, the bleeding and the weight loss along with today's symptoms, she needs to be seen in hospital straight away, not at a routine appointment.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Rigid, board-like or severely tender abdomen, high fever/rigors, confusion, fainting or collapse before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — call 000 back and report deterioration
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals perforation/peritonitis or septic/hypovolaemic shock requiring the most urgent resuscitation.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If her belly becomes rock-hard, she spikes a fever, becomes confused, or faints or collapses, call 000 straight back and tell them she is getting worse.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Frail older adult (~78) who is cachectic, dehydrated and anaemic
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower physiological reserve means faster decompensation; escalate without delay and do not attempt any remote temporising measures.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Adult child present and made the call
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Direct clear instructions to the adult child: call 000, keep her nil by mouth, keep the emesis bowl nearby, unlock the door for paramedics, and stay with her.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance right now — this looks like a possible bowel blockage, which is an emergency. Do not give her anything to eat or drink, and do not give any laxatives. Keep her comfortable with the vomit bowl nearby and stay with her until the paramedics arrive.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Call 000 immediately for an emergency ambulance
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 is available 24 hours a day — this cannot wait for clinic hours or the next in-hours period.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Provide the paramedics and ED with the history: months of altered bowel habit, rectal bleeding, ~8 kg weight loss and fatigue, now with colicky pain, bilious vomiting and absolute constipation for 2 days. The ED and surgical team will lead ongoing care and any cancer work-up.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating the presentation as ordinary constipation or a routine cancer work-up and arranging a GP appointment or laxatives instead of emergency transfer.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Ongoing complete obstruction progresses to bowel ischaemia, perforation, faecal peritonitis, septic and hypovolaemic shock and death.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Recognising urgency but routing to urgent same-day GP/urgent care rather than an ED with surgical and imaging capability while the patient cannot tolerate fluids.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Over-triage is not a meaningful risk here — the presentation is a genuine T5 emergency.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The exact cause and level of obstruction (malignant vs impaction vs diverticular vs volvulus) cannot be determined via telehealth.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Act on the emergency pattern, not the precise cause — escalate to 000/ED where imaging and examination establish the cause; keep nil by mouth.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Delaying escalation to try to distinguish the cause remotely, or defaulting to a benign 'constipation' explanation and arranging routine review.
